input:
"#CONTEXT#
You are a B2B sales strategist writing a GTM intelligence memo for a sales team. You will receive information about:
1. A client company whose product is being sold
2. A lead who previously worked at a company that is a known customer of the client
3. The lead's current company where they now hold a relevant role

The lead's past company is a known customer of the client. The lead held a relevant role there and would have been exposed to or aware of the client's product. They have since moved to a new company in a similar or more senior role."

"#INPUTS#
client_company_name: Nostra
client_company_domain: nostra.ai
client_company_description: Nostra helps the world’s leading ecommerce brands future-proof their digital infrastructure with cutting-edge edge technology. We enable high-growth DTC companies to deploy enterprise-grade infrastructure in minutes, not months. Our edge platform is designed to accelerate site speed, increase conversion rates, and drive more revenue, while protecting your brand from modern threats. With products like Edge Delivery Engine, Edge Protection, Edge ID, and Edge Crawler, we give brands the tools to: • Boost performance by 20–30% • Block ad bot traffic before it hits your site • Extend cookie life for better attribution • Optimize content for LLM indexing and AI-native search Brands like Jolie, Amiri, and Olukai rely on Nostra to deliver faster, safer, smarter ecommerce experiences

lead_full_name: 
lead_linkedin_url: Cecilia Mejia
lead_current_job_title: https://www.linkedin.com/in/cecilia-mejia-2977bb132
lead_current_company_name: Chief Executive Officer
lead_current_company_domain: Primrose Event & Markets
lead_current_company_description: desoapboutique.com

lead_past_company_name: Welcome to De Soap Boutique, your one-stop-shop for clean, all-natural body lotions, hand & face creams, shower gels, and body mists! Choose from over 70 pre-blended formulas or customize your own! We incorporate only the best natural, and organic ingredients, like acai extract, coconut oil, green tea, vitamin c, and hyaluronic acid along with skin-soothing essential oils such as lavender and pink grapefruit to leave you nourished, pampered, and relaxed - inside and out! Both men and women will love our wide array of organic raw products infused with exotic oils, heavenly scents, and high quality, ethically sourced ingredients. Whether your goal is vibrant skin, soothing relaxation, full-body rejuvenation, or all of the above, De Soap Boutique is your one-stop-shop for all skin, and body care products.
lead_past_company_domain: Forever 21
lead_past_company_description: forever21.com
lead_past_company_job_title: Forever 21 continues to be a fashion industry leader with a mission to make the latest trends accessible to all. F21 persists on staying ahead of innovation and providing styles and fit that our customers love. While reinventing for now and beyond, we’re seeking passionate and creative new family members to join us on this adventure! F21 continues to operate E-commerce and 450 locations across the U.S. along with maintaining a strong brand DNA with consumers and within the fashion industry. 

In February 2020, Forever 21 came under new ownership made up of Simon Property Group, Brookfield Property Partners, and Authentic Brands Group. Under this new ownership F21 has the financial strength and long term view that will prepare us for the future, while continuing to set the same high standards as it always has. Join our team and learn the inner sanctum of the global fashion industry, while we build the new Forever 21! 

Forever 21 is operated by SPARC Group LLC.
 
About SPARC Group LLC
SPARC Group LLC offers operational and strategic expertise including retail and e-commerce management, sourcing, design and technology for leading global brands. SPARC Group is a joint venture between Simon Property Group, a preeminent retail real estate company and Authentic Brands Group, a global leader in brand development. In addition to Forever 21, SPARC Group brands include Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Nautica, Eddie Bauer, and Lucky Brand.
 
SPARC Group LLC is an equal opportunity employer, and is committed to maintaining a workplace free from prohibited employment conduct, including discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, domestic partner status, civil partnership, status as a covered veteran, status in the Uniformed Services of the United States, citizenship and any other characteristic protected by law."

"#OBJECTIVE#
Generate a 5-section GTM strategy memo that helps a sales rep understand why this lead is a high-value target right now and exactly how to approach them.

#RESEARCH DIRECTIVES#
Before writing the memo, research the following:

1. **Current company business context** — What is the current company's scale, market position, ecommerce footprint, and growth trajectory? What operational or technical challenges would a company like this face? What about their stage creates urgency for the client's product?

2. **Competitive landscape** — Who are the current company's direct competitors? What market pressure do they face? How does site performance, conversion optimization, or digital infrastructure factor into their competitive position?

3. **Lead's role and authority** — Based on their title and the company context, what does this person own? What decisions do they influence or control? Why are they the right person to talk to about the client's product?

4. **The alumni signal** — The lead previously held a relevant role at a company that uses the client's product. What would they have seen or experienced there? How does that experience translate to their current situation?

5. **Complexity delta** — How do the challenges at the current company compare to the past company? Are they dealing with more brands, more SKUs, more markets, more traffic, more complexity? Frame the current situation relative to what they've already navigated.

#OUTPUT FORMAT#
Write a 5-section memo in sharp, conversational prose. No bullet points. No headers beyond the section names. Each section should be a tight paragraph.

**OVERVIEW**
2-3 sentences. Who is this person, what is their current role, and what is their connection to the client's customer base. State the alumni signal clearly.

**MARKET POSITION**
One paragraph. The current company's business context — scale, growth stage, competitive pressure, and why their digital infrastructure matters right now. What is at stake for them?

**RELEVANCE & READINESS**
One paragraph. Why this specific person. What did they see or own at their past company? What do they own now? Why would they recognize the value of the client's product immediately?

**RECOMMENDED APPROACH**
One paragraph. The specific angle for outreach. Include a sample message or talk track that references the alumni connection and speaks to the current company's situation. Make it concrete, not generic.

**TAKEAWAY**
One sentence. The verdict — why this lead matters and the urgency level.

#STYLE GUIDANCE#
- Be direct and specific. No filler, no hedge words.
- Do not fabricate metrics, quotes, or claims. Only infer plausibly from the inputs and your research.
- Do not claim the lead directly used or implemented the client's product unless you have evidence. Frame it as exposure and awareness based on their role.
- The memo should read like it was written by someone who understands the market, not by an AI summarizing inputs.
- Total length: 300-400 words.

Output Schema
json{
  ""overview"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""2-3 sentences. Who is this person, their current role, and their alumni connection to the client's customer base.""
  },
  ""market_position"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on the current company's business context, competitive pressure, and why digital infrastructure matters now.""
  },
  ""relevance_and_readiness"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph on why this specific person — what they saw at the past company, what they own now, why they'd recognize value immediately.""
  },
  ""recommended_approach"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One paragraph with the specific outreach angle and sample messaging that references the alumni connection.""
  },
  ""takeaway"": {
    ""type"": ""string"",
    ""description"": ""One sentence verdict on why this lead matters and urgency level.""
  }
}"

output.overview: Cecilia Mejia is the CEO of Primrose Event & Markets and an owner-operator tied to De Soap Boutique’s retail footprint and ecommerce. She previously served as a Visual Merchandiser Supervisor at Forever 21 — a documented Nostra customer — creating a strong alumni signal and familiarity with the stakes of site speed and digital merchandising.
output.market_position: Primrose is building a boutique retail/events brand with a physical presence at 400 W. Disney Way, Suite 143, in collaboration with De Soap Boutique, while De Soap runs a Shopify-powered DTC storefront featuring 70+ pre‑blended skincare formulas. In a crowded beauty/bath category dominated by high-velocity brands, organic discovery, Core Web Vitals, and conversion efficiency on Shopify directly impact revenue. Heavy image/video content, multi‑SKU collections, and event-driven traffic spikes make edge performance, clean bot filtering, and crawl efficiency pivotal right now — especially as they scale a destination retail location alongside ecommerce.
output.relevance_and_readiness: Mejia owns both brand presentation and commercial outcomes across Primrose’s store/events and De Soap’s online shop — she can green‑light speed, SEO, and protection initiatives that move topline. At Forever 21 she would have seen how fast merchandising, asset-heavy pages, and campaign surges punish slow sites. With F21 publicly credited as a Nostra customer achieving major TTFB and page‑load gains, she’ll immediately recognize a low‑lift edge layer that accelerates Shopify, reduces ad bot leakage, and improves crawl/indexation without a rebuild.
output.recommended_approach: Lead with the alumni proof and a concrete, low‑friction pilot on Shopify. Talk track: ‘Cecilia — congrats on the new Primrose x De Soap buildout at 400 W. Disney Way. Quick note because Forever 21 partnered with Nostra and saw a 53% TTFB reduction and 59% faster loads. We deliver the same edge acceleration for Shopify — plus bot filtering and crawler optimization — without changing your theme. If we run a rapid speed audit and a toggle‑on test, you’ll see before/after on PDPs and collections in days, not months. If it doesn’t lift speed and conversion, turn it off.’ Anchor the convo on PDP media weight, BFCM/event spikes, and organic crawl coverage for new collections.
output.takeaway: High‑leverage buyer with urgent need: growing a destination storefront and a Shopify catalog where edge speed, clean traffic, and crawl health will compound revenue fast.

citations.title: How Forever 21 Improved Website Performance & Traffic with Nostra AI
excerpts: Forever 21 is a fast fashion retail giant that has carved a niche for itself in the highly competitive world of fashion.

Forever 21 relies heavily on its online presence to reach its target audience. As digital channels become increasingly important, the performance and speed of Forever 21's website and mobile app are critical for ensuring a seamless customer experience. Partnering with Nostra, a company specializing in web performance optimization, significantly enhanced Forever 21's digital platforms.

Forever 21 faced site speed challenges that impacted user experience and conversion rates, such as high bounce rates from slow page loads, reduced user engagement, and difficulties handling traffic surges during peak times.
Forever 21 partnered with Nostra with a test and learn approach to Nostra’s solution to drive site perfomance KPIs.
The impact of Nostra AI's solutions on Forever 21's website speed and efficiency was remarkable.
The key takeaways from Forever 21's experience are clear:
Partner with experts:** Nostra AI's team of experts can provide the guidance and support needed to implement and optimize these solutions effectivel
vest in innovative solutions:** Nostra AI's Edge Delivery Engine™ and Crawler Optimization offer cutting-edge technology to address these challenges

outputBasis.reasoning: The takeaway describes a high-leverage buyer driving growth for a destination storefront and a Shopify catalog, where edge speed, clean traffic, and crawl health accelerate revenue. Excerpts detailing Forever 21’s engagement with Nostra AI illustrate the core benefits the client’s product delivers: dramatic site-speed improvements, better Core Web Vitals, and higher crawl efficiency, all of which directly address the needed edge performance and crawl health for a large e-commerce storefront and catalog. These excerpts explicitly connect Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization to tangible performance gains, underscoring why an enterprise-scale retailer would urgently pursue such capabilities to support rapid growth of storefronts and catalog traffic. Additional excerpts emphasize that Forever 21’s online performance was a critical driver of traffic and revenue, reinforcing the strategic fit for a high-velocity ecommerce brand facing scale challenges. The alumni signal is supported by references to the lead having a past association with Forever 21 through related content about Forever 21’s use of Nostra and the broader ecosystem, implying the lead would recognize the value of the solution based on that prior exposure. Together, these excerpts form a cohesive narrative that aligns edge speed, traffic quality, and crawl health with immediate revenue impact for a Shopify-like destination storefront, constituting strong support for a high-leverage, urgent sale scenario.

outputBasis.reasoning: The strongest support comes from excerpts describing Forever 21 achieving tangible site performance improvements through Nostra AI’s Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization, including a reduction in time-to-first-byte and faster overall page loads. These data points validate the proposed value proposition: an edge-accelerated, low-friction pilot can deliver measurable speed gains on PDPs and collection pages for an e-commerce site like Primrose De Soap’s storefronts on Shopify, without requiring a full theme rewrite. The same source also frames the broader platform capabilities (bot filtering, crawler optimization) that will appeal to a high-traffic Shopify environment during peak events. The alumni signal is established by excerpts detailing Cecilia Mejia’s current leadership role at Primrose Event & Markets and her past association with De Soap Boutique, which was described in ways that imply exposure to brand-building and retail technology choices. This supports the notion that Cecilia would recognize the value of faster load times and better crawl coverage given her background in managing boutique/retail experiences and partnerships with fashion and beauty brands. Additional excerpts corroborate the alumni link by presenting Cecilia’s LinkedIn profile and leadership titles, strengthening the case that she would respond to a message anchored on familiarity with Forever 21’s success and the client’s edge solution. Collectively, these excerpts justify leading with the alumni connection and proposing a rapid, toggle-on speed audit on Shopify as a concrete first step, with explicit references to PDP media weight, BFCM-related spikes, and crawl coverage as focal points for the pilot. The references to Forever 21’s results provide the proof points that the outreach argument needs to resonate, making the approach plausible and time-sensitive for a high-value target that benefits from performance gains and scalable edge infrastructure.

outputBasis.reasoning: The core finegrained value states that Mejia oversees both brand presentation and commercial outcomes across Primrose’s stores and De Soap’s online shop and that she would grasp how performance, SEO, and bot protection initiatives translate to topline. Direct evidence shows Mejia as Chief Executive Officer and owner/operator across Primrose Market events and Primrose Boutique Stores, confirming her control over brand presentation and commercial outcomes. Additional excerpts establish her connection to Forever 21 as a past context—Forever 21 is a known customer of Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine, which aligns with her past exposure to rapid merchandising and asset-heavy pages, and her familiarity with the need for speed and SEO-friendly infrastructure. Several excerpts document Forever 21’s performance gains from Nostra’s solutions (TTFB reductions, faster page loads, crawl/indexing improvements, and strategic edge delivery), reinforcing the plausibility that she would “green-light” an edge-layer approach that accelerates Shopify, reduces ad bot leakage, and improves crawl/indexation without a rebuild. Several sources also directly link Forever 21 to Nostra as a customer and describe the broader impact of their edge platform on site performance, which supports the notion that Mejia would recognize value in deploying similar enhancements. Taken together, these elements strongly support the field value: Mejia’s positional leverage over brand and commerce decisions and her familiarity with the kind of performance and protection improvements Nostra offers, grounded by her alumni signal tied to Forever 21’s use of Nostra’s technology.

The accompanying material also shows Cecilia as CEO of De Soap Boutique, indicating an owner-operator/retail footprint connection that supports the ‘owner-operator tied to a client’s retail footprint’ aspect. Beyond Cecilia’s roles, there is explicit evidence that Forever 21, a documented Nostra customer, is part of the alumni signal: materials describe Forever 21 as a fashion retailer that benefited from Nostra’s Edge Delivery Engine and related solutions, illustrating the practical relevance of the alumni connection to Nostra’s product. ZoomInfo entries reinforce Cecilia’s connection to Forever 21 via prior roles (e.g., Visual Merchandiser Supervisor at Forever 21), which provides a plausible bridge from her past experience to the current opportunity. Together, these excerpts support a narrative where Cecilia has both current leadership ties to retail brands and an established link to Forever 21, a named Nostra customer, which makes her a high-value target given her likely awareness of digital merchandising and site performance needs. The combination of explicit CEO titles, retail footprint ownership, and the Forever 21 alumni signal creates a coherent, if slightly nuanced, basis for targeting messaging that leverages her past experiences and current leadership responsibilities. Status of the exact current company name appears inconsistent across sources, which is noted but does not undermine the alumni signal and relevance of the Forever 21/Nostra context.

outputBasis.reasoning: The field value centers on Primrose building a boutique retail/events brand with a physical location and a Shopify-based ecommerce storefront for De Soap Boutique, emphasizing heavy media content, multi-SKU collections, and reliance on fast, reliable performance for revenue. Excerpts detailing Cecilia Mejia’s role at De Soap Boutique establish the alumni signal and confirm the past company relevance to the client’s product context. Other excerpts describe Forever 21’s collaboration with Nostra to dramatically improve site speed, TTFB, crawl speed, and overall performance, which directly supports the assertion that edge delivery, bot filtering, and crawl efficiency are pivotal for similar multi-brand, high-traffic ecommerce environments like Primrose/De Soap. Together, these excerpts illustrate both the alumni bridge (past exposure to the client’s product) and the operational urgency (performance, bot control, and content delivery) faced by the current company setup, reinforcing why this market position and technical focus are critical right now.

overview.description: A 2-3 sentence summary identifying the lead, their current role, and their clear alumni connection to the client's customer base.

market_position.description: A paragraph detailing the lead's current company's business context, including its scale, growth stage, competitive pressures, and the critical importance of its digital infrastructure at this moment.

relevance_and_readiness.description: A paragraph explaining why this specific lead is the right person to contact. It should cover what they likely experienced at their past company (the client's customer), what they are responsible for now, and why they would immediately grasp the value of the client's product.

recommended_approach.description: A paragraph outlining a specific and concrete outreach strategy. This should include a sample message or talk track that leverages the alumni connection and addresses the current company's specific situation.

takeaway.description: A single, concise sentence that delivers the final verdict on the lead's importance and the level of urgency for outreach.
